methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Blykalla, a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Janne Wallenius and Peter Szakalos, operates within a sector comprising 3 public comps (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Type One Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, and Oklo. The company has raised $60M+ to date.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method, anchors valuation to the $60M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-subsequent-stage progression in venture capital. This method yields an implied valuation of $114M. A peer-median approach was inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-set comps; while 8 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three occupy the same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier, making sector-wide medians unreliable.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $97M (low) to $131M (high), with a median of $114M. This range reflects step-up multiples spanning conservative (1.615x) to elevated (2.185x) interpretations of Series B progression. Key uncertainties include technology maturity and commercialization timeline relative to the narrower peer set, the absence of direct same-stage valuation comparables, and sector volatility in nuclear energy financing. The single-method foundation, while data-consistent, limits confidence; broader private comps or documented recent funding rounds would strengthen triangulation.
